decision_reason: r5 was already DONE (4000 steps complete, step_4000 saved to R2 before this task started). Pod GPU was idle (0% util, 0 MiB). Launching on 2nd pod would have doubled cost with zero benefit since r5 wasn't running. No r5 interrupt was needed because r5 had already exited cleanly.

config.trainer: train_alm_14b_p4.py (P4 holo variant with phi_holo gate metric)

training/train_alm_14b.py: alm14b_speedup_dataloader.diff (applied manually after fused_adamw shifted context)

patches_applied_to_pod_trainer.target: /workspace/train_alm_14b_p4.py (pod-specific P4 holo trainer, NOT the repo trainer)
patches_applied_to_pod_trainer.note: Pod runs a forked variant with HoloMapHead + phi_holo metric; patches ported manually.
applied: flash_attention_2 (env-gated via ALM_ATTN_IMPL; default SDPA after measurement)
async dataloader (env-gated via ALM_DL_WORKERS; default 0 after measurement)
torch.compile (env-gated via ALM_NO_COMPILE; disabled after 9+ min warmup with no step)
skipped_noop: disable_act_ckpt — P4 trainer never enabled gradient_checkpointing (already off)
nccl_env — applied in launcher but single-GPU, no effect

empirical_findings.finding: Audit assumed Qwen2 defaults to 'eager' attention; actually defaults to SDPA
empirical_findings.impact: FA2 +30% estimate was vs wrong baseline. On H100 seq=1024 bf16, SDPA ≈ FA2 ± noise. FA2 actually measured 1-2% SLOWER than SDPA due to kernel launch overhead on short seqs.
empirical_findings.evidence: pod Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct loaded without attn_implementation kwarg → config._attn_implementation='sdpa', attn class=Qwen2SdpaAttention

empirical_findings.finding: Audit predicted disable_act_ckpt +25%; P4 trainer never enabled it
empirical_findings.impact: Zero gain — baseline already had act_ckpt off.

empirical_findings.finding: Audit predicted dataloader async +1%; measured -2% to -4%
empirical_findings.impact: num_workers=2 with pin_memory on in-memory dataset added subprocess IPC overhead exceeding savings. Reverted to num_workers=0+pin_memory.
empirical_findings.evidence: r6 iter 1 (num_workers=2, FA2): 34.1 step/min vs r5 35.6 = -4.2%

empirical_findings.finding: torch.compile(max-autotune) exceeded 7 min warmup without producing step 1
empirical_findings.impact: Killed iter 1. reduce-overhead tried in iter 2, also >5 min without step 1. Killed iter 2. Disabled compile entirely for iter 3. torch.compile with output_hidden_states=True on Qwen2+LoRA appears to hit many dynamo graph breaks that make compile non-viable for this trainer.
empirical_findings.evidence: tmux capture-pane showed autotune cycling through 100+ triton_mm_N kernels; symbolic_shapes.py warnings

empirical_findings.finding: fused AdamW + tf32 + cudnn.benchmark + pin_memory (async H2D) collectively net ~+3%
empirical_findings.impact: Aligned with audit's conservative sum for these 3 items (~5%); actual realized gain smaller because LoRA param count (50M) makes optimizer cost small baseline
empirical_findings.evidence: r6 iter 3 (SDPA + fused + tf32 + cudnn + num_workers=0): 36.7 step/min vs r5 35.6 = +3.1%

revised_cumulative_estimate_vs_audit.reason_for_shortfall: Audit baseline was assumed eager attention + act_ckpt ON; both were wrong. Real baseline was already SDPA + act_ckpt OFF, leaving only fused_adamw/tf32/pin_memory (~+3%) and torch.compile (unmeasurable). FA2 and act_ckpt toggle together accounted for ~47 of the 50 predicted pct but both were moot.

launch_evidence.stable_step_log: [train] step=150/4000 loss=13.1302 main=12.7417 holo=0.344739 lr=3.76e-06 speed=36.7step/min ETA=105min

iteration_history: iter 1: max-autotune compile — hung >7 min, killed
iter 2: reduce-overhead compile — hung >5 min (dynamo tracing output_hidden_states), killed
iter 3: NO_COMPILE + FA2 + num_workers=2 — 34.1 step/min (-4.2% vs baseline)
iter 4: NO_COMPILE + FA2 + num_workers=0 — 34.9 step/min (-2.0% vs baseline)
iter 5 (FINAL): NO_COMPILE + SDPA + num_workers=0 + fused AdamW + tf32 + cudnn — 36.7 step/min (+3.1% vs baseline)
next_actions: Let r6 run to completion (4000 steps, ETA ~105 min from step 150)
Audit the audit: shared/state/alm14b_pt_speedup_audit_20260415.json should be annotated with empirical findings (baseline was SDPA not eager, act_ckpt was already off)
For +20% more MFU: explore seq packing (concat samples to seq=4096 instead of batch=1 seq=1024), multi-GPU FSDP, bf16 fused matmul paths, or Hopper-specific TMA/WGMMA — not in current audit scope

torch.compile investigation: output_hidden_states=True is the dynamo blocker. Alternatives: hook last hidden with forward hook instead of kwarg, or compute phi_holo on a separate detached pass every N steps
